expectations.text: Explicitly positions the customer as the hero and the brand/Isaac as the guide (not the hero)
expectations.evidence: Headline addresses customer: 'Your mission deserves a voice...' Subhead: 'We help nonprofit leaders...' Customer is the subject throughout; brand is positioned as the helper/guide.

expectations.text: Names or applies the three levels of problem: external, internal, philosophical
expectations.evidence: External ('sector-generic messaging') and internal ('the language you've inherited was built to survive funder review, not to move people') are present. No philosophical level is named or applied -- no statement about what ought to be true or what is fundamentally wrong.

expectations.text: Positions the guide with both empathy and authority (competence)
expectations.evidence: Empathy: 'You've read the empowering underserved communities sentence a thousand times. So has everyone else.' Authority: 'Say Why' is named as a methodology, and specific services (facilitation, narrative, film) signal competence.

expectations.text: Surfaces both the external problem (generic messaging) and the internal problem (how it feels to sound like everyone else)
expectations.evidence: External: 'escape sector-generic messaging' / 'sounds like every other grant application.' Internal: 'the language you've inherited was built to survive funder review, not to move people' and 'stop performing the sector and start saying the thing' -- captures frustration.

expectations.text: Includes a clear direct call-to-action, not a vague 'learn more'
expectations.evidence: Primary CTA is 'Book a Say Why Call' -- specific, action-oriented, and branded. Secondary CTA 'See recent work' is also concrete.
